task_completion.description: Split the hyperstack into channels and create max-intensity Z-projections preserving all time frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the original 5D stack split into two channels (C1 for DNA, C2 for tubulin). There should be Z-Project dialogs or resulting image windows (e.g., 'MAX_C1-mitosis.tif') that clearly show 51 frames (a time series, not a single static image). The files 'dna_max_projection.tif' and 'tubulin_max_projection.tif' should be seen saved in the results directory.

task_completion.description: Segment individual nuclei on the DNA projection at timepoint 1 using Gaussian Blur, Otsu thresholding, hole-filling, Watershed, and Analyze Particles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The DNA projection image should be visible at T=1 with a binary threshold applied (black/white). Evidence of watershed separation (thin lines separating joined nuclei) should be visible. The 'Analyze Particles' dialog or the resulting 'ROI Manager' window should be open, displaying roughly 2-8 detected ROIs.

task_completion.description: Save the detected nuclear ROIs as a ZIP file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ROI Manager should be open with multiple ROIs listed. A save dialog or file explorer should show 'nuclear_rois.zip' being saved to or existing in the ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ directory.

task_completion.description: Measure Mean, Area, and Integrated Density on the tubulin projection across all time slices using the nuclear ROIs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Results' window should be visible containing columns for Mean, Area, and IntDen. The table should have many rows (specifically N_ROIs * 51 frames). A save dialog or file explorer should show 'spindle_dynamics.csv' being saved to the results directory.

task_completion.description: Create a temporal montage from the tubulin projection showing every 10th time frame (1, 11, 21, 31, 41, 51) in a single row.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A wide image window should be visible displaying exactly 6 frames side-by-side in a single row (approx 1026x196 pixels). The frames should show the progression of the mitotic spindle. The file 'tubulin_montage.tif' should be seen saved in the results directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji GUI to perform the image processing steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show Fiji menus being opened, dialog boxes (like Z-Project, Analyze Particles, Make Montage) being configured, and image windows updating, rather than just a terminal running a headless script.

integrity.description: The generated outputs are derived from the actual Mitosis sample image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The montage image should visually depict dividing cells (spindles pulling apart). The ROI manager should contain realistic, irregularly shaped cell nuclei, not arbitrary geometric shapes. The results table should contain realistic floating-point measurement values.

integrity.description: Files are saved in the specified results directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Save dialogs or a file manager window should confirm that the path /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/ is used for all output files (dna_max_projection.tif, tubulin_max_projection.tif, nuclear_rois.zip, spindle_dynamics.csv, tubulin_montage.tif).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack)' sample has 171x196 pixels, 2 channels, 5 Z-slices, and 51 time frames. C1 (Red) shows DNA/chromosomes, C2 (Green) shows tubulin/spindle. Max-intensity Z-projection with 'All time frames' checked yields a 171x196 time series with 51 frames. Nuclear segmentation on the DNA channel at timepoint 1 should yield roughly 2-8 ROIs. Multi Measure with 'Measure all slices' on the tubulin projection produces N_ROIs * 51 rows in the Results table. The temporal montage (frames 1,11,21,31,41,51) with columns=6, rows=1 produces an image approximately 1026 pixels wide and 196 pixels tall.
